reviews.review_title: An interesting tech demo, but a letdown
reviews.review_description:
It feels like this title is essentially just a long technical demo. The story mode is excessively short, especially considering the franchise it belongs to.

**The Gameplay**

While the thieving mechanics aren't bad, the **parkour** is a huge missed opportunity; it desperately needs more freedom. As for the **combat mechanics**... it’s better not to even go there. It’s practically non-existent and way too simplistic. I understand the game rewards stealth over confrontation, but when you do get spotted, the combat feels incredibly bland.
They provide a bow and arrows, but since the game doesn't reward killing, I never used them—not even the fire arrows. I think I used the rope arrow exactly once, just for the sake of trying it.

**The Content**

The story is mediocre. You find yourself repeating the same actions across a very limited selection of maps (some are even reused for different missions). The ending was dull and uninspired; I cleared it on my first try by simply running in circles playing "cat and mouse" with the guards while deactivating stones as I passed them. Done.

**The Verdict**

The game’s strongest suit is undoubtedly the **graphics**. The immersion and environment design are impeccable—it’s a genuine pleasure to walk through these settings. But beyond the visuals, it’s just a "one and done" experience.
I expected much more from a title in this saga. **Entertaining for a moment, but ultimately forgettable.**

I'm a fan of the Thief series from the start! Some of my favourite stealth games of all time! I was excited to see someone brought it to VR. A perfect opportunity for it.
It really works hard at honouring the originals and it does a great job!
First off, I play on a 3S and the graphics are amazing! A lot of effort has been put in to the quality look of this game. The character models, the scenery, everything!
The setting works perfectly and feels like you're back in the Thief universe. Yet more immersive than ever.
The voice acting is brilliant, the main character has a warmth to her, the person who gives you missions gives a dramatic performance (sorry, I know their names but now I'm writing this review I've had a mind blank), hearing all the guards conversing and monologuing on patrol takes me back to the originals...and it is so cool to hear the return of someone and being the actual voice actor too! I won't say who, but I've probably spoiled it anyway with this. Ha. Sorry. But yeah.

It actually feels satisfying reaching out and "collecting" all the shiny baubles. It's a rush finding a dark place to hide in, and then even follow and grab the pouches from guards' belts. Opening and reaching through windows, vaulting over ledges, climbing up walls, ropes and beams...is all really immersive. Finding odd paintings and book shelves to figure out how to get behind works so well in VR and is rewarding in many ways.
You can often find numerous paths to your destination. Sometimes I find them, sometimes they make sense later, other times I completely miss them. There aren't too many but enough to really give you a sense of agency for your approach.
Knocking out the guards can sometimes be a bit clunky, but it works as well as it needs to and is a cool implementation. Though taking them out does make things easier and takes away some of the thrill and timing. They react quite well to you. And that's a thing as well, the use of noticing your voice as you actually talk is a really good addition! I often talk aloud to myself when playing games and have to remember to be quiet. Haha. And blowing a whistle to call you bird is a great touch, and even blowing to put out candles, as well as being able to snub them out with your fingers. It's so cool. We need more of this!
My main gripes for interaction and combat is that you don't get a sword, though if you did i realise that would take away the stealth focus a lot. But more so is the aiming of the bow. I find that very finicky and hard to line up the red dot. That might be a me problem but still. There were a couple times where I heard random loud and overbearing sound issues of something heavy clunking around and it wouldn't stop, yet when I looked it wasn't there. And if I reach a position suddenly guards will alert in the distance for no real reason. But they do then settle and it doesn't count as me being seen.
Overall the objectives you get, and the extra objectives really help you to want to look everywhere or even come back and do the level better.
The game is quite short, levels are quite small. I'm halfway through. But at the same time it is long enough and the levels are packed full. It takes me a good whole battery power gameplay session to finish a level fully. People complaining of length seem to forget this is all on a standalone headset. Instead of being so critical on quality games not being everything that a console game is, we need to celebrate the steps they are taking! And this game is such a good step in the right direction. And it isn't the price of a console game either.
Support this game! It is such an awesome experience!

reviews.review_title: A playful journey as thief in the night!
reviews.review_description: I've played great stuff from both Vertigo Games and Maze Theory, and Thief VR does NOT disappoint. The graphics are immersive, the gameplay is intuitive and not frustratingly difficult, the pacing of the story is good, and it's a fun adventure. Whenever I get caught up in a game, I always wish it were longer... but nothing is keeping me from going through it again and again. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: ONE OF MY FAVORITE VR GAMES!!
reviews.review_description:
I pre-ordered this game as soon as it dropped, and man, it did not disappoint! The mechanics feel so smooth, and the story is honestly incredible.I really don't get why people are hating on it. I’ve already 100% completed it three times and I still wasn't bored.

Overall, such a great game!

reviews.review_title: A decent but mixed bag of a game
reviews.review_description:
This is a tough game to review. It isn't technically a bad game yet ultimately it fails to meet its mark in several ways.

The campaign is short and there are very few, relatively small levels. However there is a certain amount of complexity to those levels which can provide the player with some choice in how to approach the end goal. And graphically it is on the higher end for Quest, although there are still some bugs here and there.

One of the biggest appeals was the immersion, which includes the ability to use the mic to blow candles out and requires the player to be quiet to avoid alerting enemies. It's a great touch but even this is let down because there are very few opportunities to employ this skill in the environment by blowing out candles, so it becomes more about the latter.

AI detection is flakey. At times I can be perched two feet away in front of an enemy and they see nothing, while at other times I'm being spotted from a much greater distance by an enemy next to a light source while I'm in the shade. This leads to false detection and sometimes outright failure because you get attacked.

You have no decent defensive capability and generally if you get caught by guards you're done for. This combines with the problematic detection to make for some very frustrating failures. For example being forced to climb through a specific window and the usually dumb guards suddenly develop bat-like hearing and attack you, ruining your stealth streak.

The bow is poorly designed for aiming as has been pointed out before, just no excuse for that.

The story sometimes breaks the game logic. You're a thief equipped with lockpicks but for *this* door you need a key, which defeats the concept of breaking locks as a professional thief.

There is no meaningful reward or upgrade system tied to your stolen goods. You're mainly doing it to check a box and this ties to the lack of replay value. Replaying levels would only be done because you feel like it, there's no incentive to do it. Additionally challenge items which are supposed to show up back at the lair are bugged, so even the cosmetic 'reward' for finding those is lost.

Having to sit through cut scenes every time you load in or start a chapter is irritating. Even more so after completion. You have to listen to the same dialogue playing out before having the chance to go select the chapter you want to replay.

These are examples of general flaws in design. It doesn't make the game unplayable or even unenjoyable (sometimes it does that), but it definitely makes it feel unfinished and unrewarding. Challenges become a gamble due to inconsistent AI and the lack of replayability makes it a relatively expensive one-off experience. 

It's worth having and playing through but could really do with a few upgrades to keep the player more interested in returning to the game.

The graphics are good, but that’s like to only good thing about it. I know that stealth is your main tool but when you have to fight, it’s is terrible. The parring is janky and when you do nock them out, the way the fall is just unrealistic. Also, the bow scope is just weird. Like it kinda jumps away from me when I’m trying to aim, also the draw back and release is janky. Also, the parkour is a HUGE missed opportunity. It’s so controlled like only being able to vault in 2 place in the entire map. You should take a leaf out of Assassin’s Creed’s parkour. So I would not recommend and I strongly discourage buying this game.

Fix some bugs especially the height problem and when crouching sometimes i cannot reach the item which gets annoying 

Also i got stuck when i ran outta arrows there are lots of kind ones but on the third chapter or fourth i think, they gave me only two rope arrows and when i missed the aim i got stuck and had to restart from the beginning 


I dont regret buying this game its actually very enjoyable and thrilling, i enjoyed stealing some stuff lol

Completely broken, a few missions the framerate is fine its stable and theres no issues or glitches but then ya get to the next mission which is a big map in comparison to ya previous one and it might be great for 50% but then suddenly the framerate tanks and its Completely unplayable, the glitches where the valuables get stick in the drawer while you try and grab em is still in the game (not fixed FYI) when the framerate tanks its nigh impossible to KO any guard with the blackjack because the framerate is that much in the gutter

The aiming with the bow is dreadful (its a simple adjustment) why haven't you done that months after release? You cannot complete the game when the framerate of your game is so unstable your moving in slow motion,,,

How much is this game full price? How is it legal to sell a broken game to millions of people? Months & months after release its still broken, but the most sickening part is the 5star reviews for this game (i expect it from corrupt paid for VR influencers lying through there teeth to get paid on the backend) but from some genuine VR players giving it 5stars why? To make yourself feel like you got less scammed then you did? 

Its broken, it was released broken and this studio scammed every person who bought this, (this is why VR is doomed to fade away) because its allowed, because its accepted, because its ignored, this studio should be blacklisted from the meta platform, this is Unacceptable, look at the state of this game months & months after release and tell me gaming overall is in a healthy place,,, 

Im sick & tired of being scammed, modern gaming is just trash & it deserves the collapse its speeding towards

This is a good game — not a blockbuster or AAA title, just a solid one. The main issue is that it doesn’t really feel like a VR game. The environment is too static, there’s almost no physics, and the combat relies on QTEs, which feels out of place in a VR game in 2026 (even if combat isn’t the main focus here). It ends up feeling too much like a port of a flat game.

This is certainly Thief. It checks the boxes. I didn't refund it because it's worth the price, well presented and not broken, and thank goodness because there is one major aspect that would have resulted in a refund if the rest hadn't been done well.

Whoever was in charge of writing and casting the main character really beefed this one. She does not even sound like a person who lives in that city, much less a real thief. Way too confident, insufferable personality, dreadful internal monologue and dialogue. She is the most immersion breaking part of the experience, and I found myself looking for a way to turn her banter off. It's like listening to a BBC character who sells flowers do a voiceover for a character she doesn't relate to. Even Garrett seemed like he was just being nice.

Without that, it comes out to a serviceable Thief game that really could have benefitted from more depth and scale and much more challenging stealth mechanics, but it gets the job done pretty respectably for 20 bucks.

reviews.review_title: Nailed it! This game is 100% awesome
reviews.review_description: Steal and loot with style. Fun to the max. Beautifully crafted. Stealth with a unique twist. Don’t miss the chance to delve into the world of Thief VR. The graphics and game controls are next gen VR style. Candlelit level design will easily immerse you into the heists, with multiple paths available throughout each setting (crafty level design). Under a full moon, light and darkness mechanics add to the atmosphere as well. 5 stars

developer_response.body: Thank you Jade for the awesome review. We're glad you enjoyed Thief VR!

reviews.review_title: Pity that the checkpoints are sketchy
reviews.review_description:
This would be great if the checkpoints w
orked - level 3 I had to replay 6 times because sometimes the checkpoints saved but not others - Level 4 & I'm on my 3rd replay - Having reached the top of the inn my progress disappeared & I had to restart it - I've lost interest in trying to find all the items which is a pity because the developers have spent a lot of time making great scenes - A few simple fixes would sort it

Fantastic game, great graphics, runs real smooth, and has phenomenal gameplay, too short for my taste with only 8 levels but lots to in each. However it has one major flaw when on day i hop in and all of my save data was deleted, even the shader settings needed to reupload. 

Mind you it was an easy fix, i had to restore an old cloud save and redo a level, but that shouldn’t be necesarry. Hopefully its fixed in a  future patch

Again, the game is probably one of the best i’ve seen in a while but almost having to restart a whole game is a pretty big issue for a game like this.

Finished playing the game tonight,
Overall I really enjoyed it. 

What I liked -

Every level is at night. Unique and nice to have it easier on the eyes 

Microphone access. Really neat to give the headset mic access (optional) which allows you to blow out candles and summon your bird friend :). Other options are full mic access so if you talk to your self and make too much noise this can alert the guards or no access at all. 

Level design. SO much to explore and uncover in every area. Can be fairly confusing as I’d double back on tracks often. 

Things to be improved 

Climbing. Pretty smooth overall but had several occasions where I’d go to grab on a selected area and it wouldent work or mid climb my hand would just stop gripping and I’d fall

Glitches/accessibility improvements

Guards. On numerous occasions I’d hit the guard to get him to drop to his knees and he’d stay standing. Hit him again to knock him out. Most of the time would drop knocked out, sometimes would stay standing. 

Some knocked out guards would imbed them selves in the ground and were not moveable 

Biggest gripe - knocked out guards snoring (please take this into consideration devs)

Knocked out guards snore continuously. Can hear them when on the other side of a wall or above/below them. I suffer from Misophonia (sound sensitivity) this almost made we wanna get a refund and stop playing. Please can there be a feature to turn that off completely or at least limit it to play for only a few seconds then stop?

Don't get me wrong, I still enjoyed my playthrough ( 5 hours with repeating some of the missions) and there are few things to like, especially exploration, music and sound effects but the rest of the game is just underwhelmed.
Cons:
Graphics ( expected more from the developers)
Too many doors and chests to picklock ( it's not challenging which makes it boring)
Way too short (. 5 hours!)and not much replayability unless you want to pick up all collectibles 
Gameplay was fine but not very challenging 
After unlocking the bow in the second mission there's nothing else to unlock apart from rewards ( perks) from completing the missions.
It's ok to pick it up on sale but don't expect much.

reviews.review_title: This game restored my faith in VR future
reviews.review_description: Thief VR has restored my faith in the future of Meta Quest Standalone games! You can tell that the developers put ALOT of work and effort into making this a polished and unique experience. I encountered game-mechanics that I never have in VR before, such as using the mic to actually blow out candles, and blow your whistle. This feature blew me away, and I think this game is up there with the likes of Batman Arkham Shadow, Ghost Town, Assassins Creed. I can't recommend this game enough! Check out my video on my YT channel :) That VR Girl

Thief: Legacy of Shadows delivers a masterclass in stealth gameplay, capturing everything fans love about the genre while adding a modern, immersive twist. From the very first mission, the game pulls you into a dark, atmospheric world where every shadow feels alive and every step matters.
The level design is outstanding, offering multiple paths and rewarding careful exploration. Whether you prefer to ghost through unseen or take calculated risks, the freedom of approach makes each playthrough feel unique. The stealth mechanics are tight and responsive, striking a perfect balance between challenge and fairness.
Visually, the game excels with detailed environments and moody lighting that enhance the tension. Combined with a subtle but effective sound design, it creates a constant sense of suspense that keeps you fully engaged.
What truly stands out is the immersion—Thief: Legacy of Shadows doesn’t just ask you to play stealth, it makes you feel like a master thief.
Conclusion:
An exceptional stealth experience that honors its roots while pushing the genre forward. A must-play for fans of tactical, thoughtful gameplay.
Highly recommended!

I think this game is great. I like that. I didn’t have to constantly swing a sword (I have a shoulder issue) just had to bop the guards a couple times. But if I was killed, it was nice that I didn’t have to go too far back in the level. Loved searching for hidden items. The physics worked great for me. I love that the lock pics weren’t too detailed. Loved sneaking around and pickpocketing the guards. so appreciated that I didn’t have to fight a big boss at the end. sorry if I gave anything away, but there are so many games I’ve had to give up on because I just can’t be the boss. A lot, a lot of work went into this game and I really appreciate what you’ve done. Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the kind review. We're glad you enjoyed Thief VR: Legacy of Shadow.

reviews.review_description:
I started with Thief of Deadly Shadows. Then whentbback and played Thief II and Gold. When the remake came out, just called Thief, I played that one too. 
 So when I tell you this has the feel and style that makes this series so great I know what I'm talking about. 
 I know some of the bad reviews have said stuff about game mechanics but as a long time fan of Thief and a long time VR player I found the mechanics easy to work with. 
 My only gripe is I would like a mode were I can duck and crawl on my own but it's not a deal breaker.

I didnt play the other Thief games but i really enjoyed this one in VR. 

Stealth, looting and checking every corner for collectables. Thats what it is all about and it did handle all of these perfectly. Its fun and I had to comlete the game in 2 days because it was so addictive.

The graphics are amazing, levels are detailed with many different paths and secrets. 

Fighting is in the game as well but only as a last resort when you fail while hiding in shadow. You want to avoid swordfight and rather use your bow with various arrow types.

The aiming with bow is the only thing i could complain about as you need to have the bow in front of you. But is not so big deal.

Overall it is 9,5/10 game for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review! We're glad you enjoyed Thief VR

reviews.review_description: As a original player of the first Thief on pc, I was delighted when they decided to do it for VR... The game on quest 2, even if not that impressive graphically, is amazing to play! The original way of playing is a slam dunk for VR, I love the storyline and the physics are great! The add of a mic for real sound and blowing candle is a nice touch!  When I will upgrade my PC I will enjoy the better graphics but I am so glad you made it also native.  Great games never die... I hope you would do the same for a VR adaptation of "Dishonored" which would be perfect for VR too. 5/5... Thank you!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review!

reviews.review_description: This is a great game, although it's less difficult than I thought. However, I'm wondering if it's possible to find other players to ask for advice. I'm stuck because I can't open a fake library. However, I hope to find more chapters of this fabulous game.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you very much for your kind review. We'd love to clarify the save issue you are having. At certain points during the levels, you will reach save points. If you open the pause menu and select exit mission, your in-game level progress will be lost. A message will appear confirming this if you select exit mission. If you bring up the Meta menu by using the controller (meta button) and quit the game that way, it will save your progress. Sorry that you had to restart the mission a couple of times, but I hope this helps for future levels.

reviews.review_description: Love the game. Graphics are some of the best I've seen. Movement is great. I love games that have side goals and collectibles to search for. After the first patch I didn't have any more bugs or glitches. I went through and played it again. Glad I pre-ordered. It was fun and I played ALOT! Made sure I got all the badges for each level. That was fun! Great game!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the positive review!

reviews.review_title: THIEF VR IS GREAT ON QUEST!
reviews.review_description:
🚨SEARCH ‘GAMERTAG VR’ ON YOUTUBE FOR MY FULL REVIEW🚨

Theres 6 chapters with 5 sandbox styled levels and these levels are filled with various ways to go either obvious or hidden. Theres tons of items for you steal and you have multiple objectives to try and complete either primary or secondary and your mastery thief performance is rewarded with perk cards at the end of every mission.

The multiple level approach the devs have marketed for thief is pretty real too. You can play this simply by clubbing everyone or killing them if you have enough arrows or you can try to sneak around an entire level which will easily double your play time.

Ratings Edit
Since the 4th patch things have much improved so Im changing my rating from a 2 star to a 4. There are still some bugs to found but most of the big problems such as the bow mechanics and doors misplaced( to be found sticking out of walls at odd angles etc) have been  fixed and the game is very enjoyable !
Most of the game mechanics are rock solid and the thrill factor is certainly there.
In my opinion a few things still need to fixed, such as dialogue spontaneously happening completely out of no where, especially if you reload the last checkpoint.
The odd guard is marching around about a foot off  the ground and occasionally mantling through a window sometimes requires a little finnesing as the mantle prompt doesn't show up especially if you're standing and you then need to crouch.
The occasional piece of loot can get " stuck" through the base of a draw but a little perseverance can get it out.
Also sometimes some "dark" areas become very bright with a pale grey fog obscuring the view but this maybe down to the led screens trying to simulate a very dark area and we are seeing the screens backlight. 
But these are just niggles compared to the previous problems and I now highly recommend this game. If the devs can fix the last of the problems that still exist I'll happily change 4 stars to 5 !

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sticking with Thief VR throughout the updates. It really means a lot that you continued to give the game a chance and we are pleased you find the game very enjoyable. We would love to find out more about the issues you are having so we can continue to enhance the game and make it even more enjoyable. Please feel free to submit a ticket if you ever get 5 minutes https://support.eidosmontreal.com/hc/en-us/categories/31424726119709-Thief-VR-Legacy-of-Shadow or join the Vertigo or Maze Theory Discord where we also monitor support for Thief VR.

reviews.review_description:
I have 11+ years in VR gaming.  Here's my review.

This feels like a Developer's 1st Crack at a VR game.

You can't crouch in real life. Feels like the game is locked to a camera that is only 1 set height at a time and it happens to also be your face.

The bow weapon. Mastered by almost all VR games that have it. For years. Except this one you can't aim unless you oclude one controller with the other, which makes your Quest 3 start drifting the front hand because it can't see it. And the holding angle is wrong there, too.

These aren't bugs, theyre poor design choices for VR. We're here to be immersed, live the experience. Unfortunately,  doing normal things here doesn't work well. Again, bad design. I loved the original Thief, and I'll keep it good in my memory by not playing this until there's some updates.

Personal note: im so very bored of 2D devs riding a hype train off of previous talent to push a crap VR experience. I don't know if that happened here or not, I didn't even look to see who made this. But judging by what im seeing  in the game i feel obliged to bring it up.

I love this game, but I have some serious complaints which I wrote to the developers about and the most important one STILL hasn't been resolved.

SNAP VIEW CROUCH ABSOLUTELY SUCKS!

Any and EVERY decent VR and flat game smoothly transitions the camera into a couch view... NO EXCEPTIONS!

Other than that huge disappointment and a few glitched doors, This is an great VR version of the Thief series. I wish the city was traversable, though. That and the lack of the ability to buy items also left a lot to be desired. I loved the Thief series, ever since Deadly Shadows and enjoyable part of gameplay was the fact that getting to a mission, was a mission in and of itself. I wanted to give this game a 5 star rating, but when I separated my loce for the franchise and, as unbiased as possible, compared it to other great VR games, I would give it a 3.5, if i could. Luckily for Magpie, a 3 is too low. It's still a great game!!! I hoping for DLC and PLEASE FIX THE CROUCH CAMERA TRANSITION!!!!

reviews.review_title: Really wanted to play this, but…
reviews.review_description: Thief looks like something I’d genuinely enjoy, but I can’t play it because there’s no teleport option. It’s listed as “comfortable,” yet the movement style just isn’t accessible for me. What makes it more frustrating is that Vertigo Games titles are usually ones I can play without any issues — this time, it’s the opposite. I wish I could experience it, but the locomotion choice makes it a no‑go.

The game has blown me away for a new game. The reason I got this game was because if had a wonderful time playing other games from vertigo studios and this one popped up and I had to get it. Since the game is new there have been a bit of things slightly bad like the enemies were hitting me from very far away and they weren’t to realistic but I think they will get patched in upcoming updates so don’t worry. Also, I am not a big puzzle game guy, but this one pulled me in and is amazing. The game gives me a bit of a thrill sneaking around and stealing stuff but not to bad. The story is also pretty good and I would recommend trying at least cause it kinda incorporates every single game style. Overall five star game and I really recommend getting it and maybe trying other games from vertigo ￼￼￼￼￼￼studios. I hope this helped you in your purchase , and that’s my review on this game.
A little short but it has replay value to get all the loot so I won't complain to much.  Overall though it's great like all there games.

Don’t even breathe, or the guards will hear you!
Playing the game without the distraction of other people in the room is important, turning on the option for enemies to actually hear you, is equally important to fully appreciate this game.
Graphics are beautiful at times, always sharp and serviceable. 
Sound design is a bit of a mixed bag, with ok sound effects, atmospheric music and good voice .
That said, the main characters inner dialogue is something that should have it’s own option to mute in the options menu. 
The game is not difficult… to combat this, try avoiding combat altogether, it adds depth to the gameplay and can unlock new skills for the player.
After the first tutorial mission that is bit restrictive the game gets more complex and fun.
Although the games biggest weakness is constantly there, virtually blind and ridiculously stupid enemy ai, this needs to be improved, doing so would make it a near perfect game. All in all, if you already played the superior Assassin Creed: Nexus, why not try Thief VR?

I really liked this game but it is so short. I would have expected there to be three times as much gameplay. I found this disappointing as this has potential to be an amazing game. I’m glad I  never paid full price.
Aiming the bow is very strange  as you have to aim add an odd angle.  Overall, the game needs work but has potential to be something special especially for thief fans.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We're sorry you found the game too short. Attempting to complete all secondary and reward objectives on each level will increase gameplay length if you are looking to replay the game.

reviews.review_description:
One of my all-time favorites. It took me 12 hours to get through the first time. And then I started it a 2nd time and got through in 8 hours. I love that you can redo levels to collect more gold and do better. It's addicting and I keep coming back to play. I absolutely love trying to figure all of that out and what are the best ways to get around, or knock out the guards. 

5's across the board! Love it!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the positive review!

reviews.review_description: I would have liked to rate this game higher, but I was unable to continue playing due to insufficient visibility. Despite adjusting all available headset settings, the brightest option only resulted in increased visual fog, making continued gameplay unfeasible. Many other games offer in-game brightness adjustments, and I hope this feature will be implemented in a future update.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review. We're really sorry you are having trouble with the visibility of the game. We would really like to help you out and see if there is anything we can do. Please can you send a support ticket to https://support.eidosmontreal.com/hc/en-us/categories/31424726119709-Thief-VR-Legacy-of-Shadow or join the Vertigo or Maze Theory Discord server, where we will be able to help.

Are there any specific areas that you find too dark? We can investigate this internally.

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: When i next tried to play the game wouldn’t even load. I uninstalled and reinstalled the game. The same issues persist elsewhere. Sneaking around, picking locks, finding secrets is fun.  Parrying is still awful so best if it’s avoided, especially with the guards with battle axes. During 1 mission i basically got stuck in the crouch position. When I stood I was about an inch taller. I opened option and tried to recalibrate height. You click it and nothing happens. Tried repeatedly. Since I couldn’t reach anything I couldn’t progress and had to quit and start the mission all over again. The next run the right hand again was functioning but no white dots would appear when attempting to interact with the environment and you can’t play the game with only one hand (its not a controller issue). So I had to quit and start again, each time needing to listen to the whole dialogue in the clocktower. Not sure why I couldn’t just start the mission. A couple small glitches with not being able to interact with white lines to climb and a couple other things. For the last mission I finished the observatory, got rewards, started the ending with the altars and quit to finish later. When i went back the only option was replaying the entire mission, which i wasn’t going to do so I never actually got to finish the game. Not sure why I couldn’t do pick up where I left off when it was basically a new mission. 

1st REVIEW 2 stars: I’ve only made it through the first mission. The parrying part in the tutorial froze so I restarted. Nothing was happening but it eventually played out. During the first mission, my right hand would flex but couldn’t grab anything, making climbing difficult. Parrying hardly works and i get hit a lot. Then in the shop i got the bow, grabbed with my right hand and it would never let go. When i reached in my backpack with my left hand it grabbed a bow. So now i have a real bow and a ghost bow in my hands. I could not grab anything with my right hand and therefore couldn’t get any arrows to proceed. Now Im stuck. Hope this is fixed soon.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that you are experiencing issues. Just to rule out hardware-related issues, could you please try restarting your headset and check if you also have problems with the right hand in other games?
If this does not help, please reach out to our customer support so we can assist you further with the issue: https://to.plaion.com/ThiefVR-Support

reviews.review_description: i have loved the thief franchise since i was first introduced to it with Thief deadly shadows, and although i never played the thIf pc games, i got enamored by the dark and gothic atmosphere of the game. then i played thief 2014 and i was surprised with the way the game played out and, i was always wondering about the next installment in this legendary franchise. i have never played vr before but as soon as i noticed Thief VR was announced i knew i had to play it and then i bought my meta quest 3. this game is a masterpiece for stealth gamers and vr players alike. everything about it is great: the music, the gameplay, the characters, and the city itself. this is a must buy for not only if gamers but also for all the VR players it is a must buy it is incredible. i hope we get a DLC or a new game sooner.

reviews.review_title: aSo far, so good love all the characters
reviews.review_description: At first, I thought this game was just gonna be oh Perry this then Perry that sneak this oh no that guard sleeping be as quiet as you can but then the second level really really made me have to think that’s what I love about games the challenge for the rewards. I’m still stuck on the second level though I’m still trying to figure it out. I would recommend downloading this game because if you wanna be a sneaky thief or a quiet, but definitely OK that sounds gross anyways I think the developers worked very hard on this game and they did awesome

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. We're glad you are enjoying the game! Please feel free to reach out in the Discord server if you would like any help completing the levels.

reviews.review_description:
Everything below accurate as of 03/01/2026:

Started out in this having a great time once I got it actually running (it never loads for me on first boot and I have to restart my headset every single time, otherwise just sits forever in that purple loading void they put in the quest 3), but ran into a few bugs of items not populating in containers, then going back later and finding them full of things to steal. Also had several occasions of big black voids in doorways and windows, which looked at first as if the level beyond hadn't loaded in, but turned out to be just razor thin sheets I could just walk or stick my head through. Then I got to the 5th mission and had the game close no less than 5 times in a row over and over again. Seems like it is, design-wise, a great game, but needs desperately to be fixed. At the minute I would say don't buy. Maybe you'll never hit these bugs, but I ran into all of them quite frequently and the 5th mission crashing bug has effectively rendered it unplayable.

Outside of these issues, I was having a fantastic time with this game. The climbing, sneaking, and stealing are all a hell of a lot of fun, and the level design as you progress further becomes nice and open. Just wish I could get past New Quarter without the game closing on me.
Real nice hearing Garrett again too.

Update: 06/01/2026
So it seems as though this game is a little too easy to sequence break. Turns out because I didn't go straight to the mission objective at the beginning of New Quarter, when I tried to make my way through the inn, it just crashed. Restarted level and followed the story exclusively and it didn't crash, but has basically put me off exploring. Then got to the following level and somehow ended up crashing that level too so I have given up playing until there is another patch.

developer_response.body: Hi. Thank you for playing and sharing your feedback. Apologies for any problems you've encountered with the game. As part of our effort to improve the game we've just released a new update that addresses many player-reported issues as well as makes various improvements and optimizations to the game.

reviews.review_description: An incredible experience from start to finish. Had a couple of glitches here and there, but none that diminished my enjoyment. Everything from the visuals to the voice acting to the mechanics are top notch. Story was a little expected but I think it stands up well with the rest of the franchise.

•	It’s a short game with only 4–5 levels
	•	It gets boring quickly
	•	Once you finish it, you’ll probably delete it soon
	•	After finishing, there’s nothing to do except replay the levels”

developer_response.body:
Hi David,
Thank you for sharing your honest feedback. The levels are designed to be deeper than a single playthrough and invites replayability. Each level has a variety of missions to complete, which award badges, and even hidden artefacts that drastically extend the playtime and challenge

reviews.review_description: This is the start of a good game I had it on preorder stayed up the whole night   I started today a few hours ago I could see where it's going and it looks great but can we get the arrows to aim correctly if we are supposed to hit them in a head   and that's the only way to kill them using those arrows can we get the arrows to hit them and have some type of damage other than a head t cuz it's very it's extremely hard to hit them in the head I understand that it needs brushing up .can we get hit a few more times before we die ... 😂... Excuse I'm a professional theif I know what I'm doing.i think they are funny when they talk ..this place is made for watching!!    the game is great I see the vision I'm not even worried about the graphics I waited my whole life to play this game .can we get it more smoother.i believe in this game it's a great start .    That guy said hold on some one knock you smoove out !! 🤣🤣🤣.thanks for making this game I LOVE YOU

reviews.review_title: Good game with a couple issues...
reviews.review_description:
Just finished the game. Enjoyed it. Story, atmosphere, game play mechanics all work well. I would have given it a 5 but I ran into a couple issues.

1. The level where your suppose to retrieve the mask was a complete mess. The frame rate was horrible the entire level. Very janky and barely playable. Everything ran fine before and after that chapter. 

2. Collecting items out of my bag would not register right away. I'd have to hold my hand over the item for like 5-6 seconds before it would let me grab it. Hopefully a patch can fix these issues.

Besides that, everything else was really cool. Had fun playing it.

(Played on Quest 3)

Patch 4 Feedback:

* The height calibration is still broken. (Note that I play standing.)

* Some collected relics are missing from the clock tower.

* The graphics update seems to have introduced a new glitch. Occasionally a patch of large white pixels appears briefly.

After Completion:

It would have been wise to wait to see if the developers finish the game before finishing the story but I continued to struggle past all the jank and bugs because the game is just too compelling.

Save bugs. Quest items disappearing after being found. AI glitches. The list of issues is too long to list here.

Despite all that after completing the final mission I immediately went back and replayed the first mission to try to finish all the optional objectives.

5 stars for the art direction and game design. One star for putting it up for sale while still in development. I'll give it a 3 for now but hopefully I can give it a 5 someday if they ever get it working right.

Update:

Don't buy this unless you enjoy working as a beta tester without pay. The performance degrades the longer you play it. The height calibration issue is just the start of height problems. I had to quit out and reload the game several times because I'd end up getting lower and lower each time I crouched until I was the height of a small child.

The performance degraded to the point where the frame rate was choppy. Then the game crashed. I tried reinstalling and it deleted my save. I've spent almost an hour trying to restore an older save from the cloud.

First Impressions:

I've only played for around an hour so this isn't a full review but just first impressions.

I'm a history buff and I feel like I've already gotten my money's worth out of Thief just by being in the beautiful historically inspired fantasy world they've created.

The game mechanics work well if you're not expecting realism. This plays like a game, not a simulation and I'm enjoying every minute of it.

Well, almost every minute. The height calibration doesn't work.  If you bend down to pick something up off the ground your hand stops around six inches short. I've tried clearing the boundary data. Sometimes I have to use the crouch button. Hopefully this glitch will be addressed in a patch.

Based on what I've played so far I'll likely be playing through it more than once.

I absolutely loved this game. It took me around 8/9 hours to complete, but once completed, you can go back and do the challenges you didn't finish. I didn't have any of the issues others are saying they had with puzzles not working, etc.

I was fully immersed in the Thief world, hearing the voice of Garrett was a very nice touch. I also loved how you could blow out the candle and blow the whistle by actually blowing in real life. As someone who plays so much VR, it was great to experience something new. I liked all the different types of arrows you could use. Plus, if you did the challenges set to you, like finding the relic, you could upgrade your skills.

Graphic wise, I thought it looked great. The storyline was fine. It was extremely well voice acted. My only disappointment was the ending. I won't say anything more on the ending to avoid spoilers. Definitely in my top 5 VR games I've played this year.

really great game my second favorite in fact but! This game I Very sad! So be weary (Spoilers ahead) THE FREAKING BIRD DIES IT MADE ME SO SAD😭😭😭 but it comes back thankfully so no worries here 10/10 game would recommend!

Thief VR: Legacy of Shadow had everything going for it. The franchise pedigree, a perfect genre fit for VR, and genuinely impressive first two missions that showcased solid stealth mechanics and immersive level design. Then everything fell apart.

Even after two patches, the game remains plagued by broken systems that destroy player trust. Bookcase puzzles break consistently. Frame rates plummet in later levels, inducing motion sickness. Traversal mechanics vacillate between smooth and clunky, shattering immersion when mounting ladders or climbing through windows becomes a struggle. The checkpoint system is unreliable, sometimes sending you back to the beginning of lengthy levels.

The stealth system itself is laughably broken. The binary lighting model means you can stand directly in front of guards, brightly lit, and remain invisible as long as the game registers you as "in shadow." This isn't stealth, it's comedy. Spatial audio is misplaced, making you worry about guards that don't exist.

Beyond the bugs, the game lacks depth. Six short levels with minimal variety, embarrassingly stupid AI, and an underwhelming narrative with an anticlimactic ending. Stephen Russell returns as Garrett but feels criminally underused.

The tragedy is that the strong opening creates false hope before the refund window closes. What could have been a decent immersive sim becomes a lesson in how not to release a VR game.

For our full review, check the 6DOF Reviews website or our YouTube Channel!

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thanks for your review. Sorry you had a less than positive experience with the game. We have been releasing updates for the game that address the issues you mentioned. The bookshelf puzzles have been patched and yesterday's update specifically addressed the performance issues in the New Quarter mission that we received complaints about.
Hope you'll try it out now and let us know your updated impressions.

reviews.review_description: Has so much potential I’ve never had a game with so many glitches it’s pretty much unplayable very disappointing

I go the game last night, and I really enjoy it as of now! Some parts are a little laggy and there are some bugs however so far it’s been a lot of fun to play. I wouldn’t say it’s as good as Metro awakening ( Best VR game ever made in my opinion) but still a lot of fun! Vertigo games is by far the best company to make VR games.

Previously, I pointed out a bug where rewards weren't received after clearing Chapter 3 missions, but this time it was properly fixed. Thank you for the quick fix.
However, now there's a new bug where, after clearing Chapter 3 missions, you somehow end up getting all 14 rewards.
Getting rewards you shouldn't is better than not getting them at all, but I still think this should be fixed.
Still, the game is genuinely fun. I did give it just three stars once, but since they fixed it this time, I want to give it a fair five-star rating.
It would be even better if enemies acted more unpredictably.

reviews.review_title: Game is good for stealth READ DESC
reviews.review_description:
So thief legacy shot was I will say stealth is amazing as well because it’s in VR there’s not that many stuff titles only so title that comes to my mind is assassins Creed but the game is very fun. It’s got very good immersive features for example being able to pick locks Picking paintings for example getting the lots of paintings doing bookshelf puzzles pickpocketing guard the game is very good being able to use the blackjack find is really good. The game is very slow paced though and is a true game to stealth
. It’s not like this on the web if you get spotted you can go home. You take the guys out. The game is very old school and very stealthy when we personally I could see someone playing this game here and there not a game that you continuously are playing it for hours and hours upon end I didn’t know myself of course standing for long periods of time, I’m playing the game But end of the day I did find the game very good. They are some cons about this game, especially being a matter it might not look aesthetically the best, but the game was try his best. I will wait till the update comes out. ￼

reviews.review_title: Good enough Deebo Sim, but not Thief sim
reviews.review_description:
Where to start with this one?
Small negative things:
1. Height calibration is worthless. Even if you calibrate it for the shortest or tallest person, you will still not be able to reach the floor if you crouch.
2 No control swaps. It is not as important since the items in the game can be picked up by either hand but still annoying. I would like to use the right thumbstick instead of left.
3. Guards (unlike in other Thief titles)  armor or not, too easy to knock out. In previous titles, you would not be able to blackjack an armored guard in full plate armor.
4. Guards, despite being justifiably tired at their night patrols, are unbelievably stupid. You could sneak past them, almost at their nose in the bright light.
5. In previous Thief titles, if you couldn't find the key, you could pick the lock. In this Thief title, you are forced to find the key since the missions are much shorter, and they're all story missions, with no side quests.
6. In previous Thief titles, the game was more of the open- ish world (open city). Here, you can only replay the previous missions.
7. Stolen goods infinite capacity. This was probably one of my biggest pet peeves/let downs. In previous titles, you couldn't just go on the klepto spree by looting the entire mission/world/open city map. The Thief was more of the management game (which is fun since being such prominent cat burglar requires careful planning and management,  and not just walk in and Deebo the whole place). Inventory of the backpack was shared with the Thief tools. You had to choose to either risk to go on the heist prepared, but with limited space of what valuables or go with the empty bare essentials, but have plenty of space to klep to your desire like a kid in the free candy store.
8. Stupid upgrades. You earn talent points and not the upgrades to your tools or skills, and money is meaningless. I'm previous Thief titles. You didn't upgrade just the character, but also the equipment from the Fence. Do you want a more powerful bow (such as composite bow instead of the simple hunting bow)? That will be 1000 gold. Wall climbing hook claws? 500 gold. Add a sight to your bow? Maybe 250?
 Bringing more gold = better and more of the much needed gear for future heists/missions. 
In this Thief? You gain "One talent point" for stealing a certain amount of threshold.

Overall I didn't feel like Magpie or Garret from previous titles, but more like Deebo, who kept walking up to guards and do the one - two Blackjack KOs and yell "That's my Gold B$%@$! Imagine this 120 lb little Magpie Deeboing 3 fully armored and armed to the teeth guards in the courtyard. Yeah.... no, thank you. It's bare bones Thief and that's about it.
Enjoyed it from beginning to end. Hope it becomes a series on vr as well! Will definitely play again!

I have loved Thief from the beginning and played the first edition as a PC Game when it was out probably back in the 2000s if not before! 

The VR experience exceeded my expectations. I am so impressed with how they have created this environment. It's so great to play this game in a fully immersive experience.

reviews.review_title: Being a Thief in VR is awesome
reviews.review_description:
Just finished the game and these are my thoughts. 
1. Visually beautiful on Quest.
2. Loved every single thing about this game. 
3. A few bugs, but nothing that stops performance or being able to move forward through the game. 
Great Job guys. 💕

reviews.review_title: Great to see a Legend return
reviews.review_description:
Like 7th Guest, Vertigo has brought another legend back from the dead and into VR.

Its great to return to the city again, though brief, I hope that there may be a sequel in the future.

Did encounter a few glitches and fought the vaulting and bow controls a bit but other than that it was a fun experience and I my head cannon habits nicely in between Thief 3 and 2014.

Vertigo Games is known for delivering high-quality experiences, and Thief VR: Legacy of Shadow is no exception. After the action-heavy gameplay of After the Fall and Arizona Sunshine, it’s refreshing to see the studio tackle the stealth genre—and they absolutely nailed it.

This is not a point-and-shoot experience. The game fully embraces stealth, encouraging you to move quietly, observe your surroundings, and think like a true master thief. The medieval atmosphere is rich and immersive, the game mechanics are unique and well thought out, and the execution is solid from start to finish.

Missions are varied and engaging, pushing you to plan your approach carefully and steal valuable objects without being noticed. The reward system is well balanced and adds meaningful replayability, making each successful heist feel earned. At times, you genuinely feel like Arsène Lupin in VR.

Thief VR: Legacy of Shadow is perfectly suited for virtual reality and does an excellent job of making you feel clever, cautious, and deeply immersed in the role of a thief. A must-play for stealth fans.

I have a quest 2 and i can tell that this game is a blast. The graphics are not bad amd it runs pretty smoothley even thoe it crashed once for me but tahts was my fault. And the  fact that you can complete the missions in diffrente ways is crazy. Still i havent played it trought but i hope they planing to ad more missions and maps or some sort. But devs pls keep it up this is a great game.✌️🥹

This is from the perspective of someone who played the original Looking Glass titles and like many were really hoping this would be a triumphant return given how well VR suits the genre. 

But it's all come off a little as wrapping a Thief brand around a more generic, rushed game I feel.

It's like that movie you look forward to seeing based on the trailer. The opening 15mins are intriguing before the entire plot if revealed and you endure the downslope for rest of the movie of bad acting, horrid pacing and crap story.

The spatial audio is way off frequently.  

The use of bow feels very arcadey compared to how we've seen it implemented well in other games.

 The levels look kind of steam punk but brightness is too high and there is a lack of dread and consequence to the whole storyline. 

The story is ...weak. Character never develops, learns new information or skills and finale is the worst part. It's rushed, it abandons any pacing of story and even the mechanics of entire game. It leaves a bad taste. 

Interactions are mostly good so it's not a bad attempt by any means on that front but it could be so much more.

In terms of sneaking around and detection, it has to be much better than that of a 1998 PC title and yet it doesn't manage to do so.

And the UI needs to have an "experienced" mode to turn off all the indicators. I had to shut as much as I could off relying on controller vibrations for lockpicking, and removing frames etc to try and improve immersion.

It looks like thief, plays sort of like a thief game (sneaking and looting) but it feels a bit hollow like Thief 2014 did for fans. Actually, 2014 is better for sure.

I really hope it does well enough to warrant another go at it with more ambition. It's an ok attempt but it needs a lot more depth and polish. It's the little things devs as especially for Thief fans all the looting should enable equipping, not some end of level rewards like a mobile game. 


It's just.....meh

you're a Thief in a mysterious and rich alternate medieval steampunk world where tech meets arcane. All thieving activities are natural VR material: climbing, lockpicking, opening doors, drawers and chests after previous loot. If guards are alerted, you run and hide, you're not a warrior. 

game is pretty smooth, looks great, runs great and is bug-free - except the legendary relics I collect seem to disappear from the trophy cases at the clock tower. Should be an easy fix.

I was enjoying the game until the third chapter
 where I completed everything but when I reached the gate where I was supposed to leave to exit the level there is a spiked gate in front of it and I can't find any help anywhere! I don't see it on any walkthrough I've completed every task and sub task required I've done everything possible still spiked gate in front of the exit!! After playing for over an hour and having to start over every time I play I am beyond frustrated!!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for playing.
Every spiked gate has a power source that needs to be turned off to lower them. In the case of the one at the level exit, there is a generator in a shed on one side of the level (hint: you need to steal a key to get in).
Hope this helps and you keep enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description: There has to be a quest 2 specific issues or something. If everyone's playd exactly what i playd there would not be a single review over 1 star. I couldnt even get past the tutorial because the combat was so bad. I just got done with behemoth on the hardest difficulty so ive gotten really good with parrying in vr and could not get it done in this game outside of the slow-mo tutorial. Even sneaking up on a guard i bashed the guy in the back of the head a dozen times before he turned around and killed me. I really hope they can figure out how to fix this. I was really excited for this game because metro awakening also done by vertigo is my number 1 favorite vr game, i had a ton of fun with it. With the limited gameplay i was able to complete i have a really hard time believing anyone that worked on metro awakening worked on this game at all.

Well the loading screen was peaceful. Unfortunately that’s all I was able to see. Waited 10 minutes to download the game and then 5 minutes on the loading screen. Thought it had froze so I restarted the app, only to wait another 5 minutes for it to load before finally giving up. Couldn’t even try to play the game. Shame on the devs. Meta quest 3 should work perfectly if I just spent $30 on a game.

Firstly thank you to the developers for eventually fixing the black triangle on recorded footage, although it would have been nice if that had been acknowledged and as promised I have returned to leave an updated review.  This is an okay game, nothing super special, some nice mechanics and generally not bad for around 4 to 6 hours, although the ending seems to almost play itself, which is rather anticlimactic. There are still some very strange issues, but I won't go into them too much here (all the crazy stuff is as always  included in my playthroughs), but twice I had the game going into a weird stuttering slow-motion, which required reloading checkpoints to fix, I was awarded every single in-game buff very early on (after the 2nd mission I believe) and climbing over walls/through windows was very hit and miss, but yeah it is an okay game, I personally wouldn't rave about it, but it was nice for a few hours... Stroking the bird is the best bit!

This game is easily in my top 10 list of best VR Games. Granted there are a few bugs, and a few rough edges, but I think thats forgivable,  the guys did a great job of bringing the Thief series to VR.  I think it's a great game, but too short (8hrs to get to last level), it would have benefitted  with a few more levels, and the addition of a few mechanics that were in the original game series (the sound effect when you steal something, fences, breaking doors, and moss arrows).

Great game! I played half an hour and it definitely lives up to the hype. The mechanics are great and intuitive. Pro tip:  instead of using buttons to crouch, try crouching physically, next level immersion!

reviews.review_title: Love the game but its not perfect
reviews.review_description: Loving the game so far but it has some issues. Like why does it randomly make me the height of a toddler? And the bow sucks to use, luckily so far I haven't had a real reason to use it. Also going into places where you have to duck if you stand up you often clip out of bounds. But aside from these issues this is a great game

Edit 2: Okay I finished it. THIS IS THE BEST STEALTH GAME. But the framerate is too stuttery, the guards are too easy, and the game is a little shy on content, with a handful of maps that get reused. But by the end I was so good at beating the guards there was no more tension.

There needs to be a hardcore mode where the guards can see / hear better, maybe alert each other, the whoosh noise is disabled, and if you die you lose your save position. 

There should also be a "touchless" play style where you never strike / harm / fight with a guard. Just sneak!

Fix the framerate and give us more difficulty options, and I will replay / increase my rating happily. The framerate is the big problem.



Edit 1: Oof the frame rate is killing me on the third level (mask). Finally got through it, and now the fourth level seems a lot smoother. Devs, please optimize and / or reduce the load!!!

I have wanted this game since VR happened and it delivers. Just drained my battery on it and lost a couple hours. So good! No game-breaking bugs yet. 

Let's keep the ratings up and support the devs! :)

DEVS: 

- Make the discord easy to find on your website. :)

- Give us an option to disable the "whoosh" noises when people start to notice us. This should be my responsibility :).

- The guards are not scary because I know I can beat them. Give us a hardcore option where one sword hit kills us. Or make them swing faster / notice us easier. 

- I am also generally not scared because the checkpoints are so close. Leave the checkpoints so we can save / leave / come back later, but make a hardcore mode where death resets us to the start of the level. Rising stakes = more excitement :)

- On the smooth levels it is beautifully smooth but still stutters every few seconds, feeling like a buffer issue or a resource load.. Takes some of the immersion out and makes me a little ill. Can you lower the graphics or something to smooth it out?

- I often make a huge noise just stepping down stairs or off a short obstacdifficulty have fallen 5 feet. Maybe have a minimum fall height to trigger this.

- Just remove the magpie quiet grunt when vaulting, or use a breathing noise iNstead of turning a loud effort noise down (sounds weird and far away).

- In the basement of the second level in the underground vault there are drawers that when you open them there is the image of another drawer inside. Through the darker door off that room, there is a chest that is invisible. I could see the items floating but had to pick a lock to get them.

- have the inventory only stay open while holding the button, consistent with the rest of the UI and object interactions. ;)

Fantastic game so far. I absolutely love it!!!

Update: Originally was very pleased with the graphics and gameplay but frame rate drops in the later levels stopped progress; the devs have sinced patched it and the game is now fantastic to play!



Was absolutely loving this until about the 4th level where the framerate becomes like a childrens pop up book.  Makes it very uncomfortable to play and spoils whats otherwise a fantastic game. Its a shame because graphically it looks incredible on quest and its an overall fun experience; I hope the framerate bug can be patched and I will easily give 5 stars when that happens
I think I would really like it if it had teleportation

When I hopped into this game, i instantly liked it. I already play VR games for a while but I personally think this game is better dan Batman: Arkham shadow. And yeah, there are indeed some glitches in this game but it’s still in early access and I don’t have any problems with these little glitches. The game still gets some updates to fix these tiny glitches so I have no problems with it at all. I personally think the glitches are pretty fun. Overall, I highly recommend this game, it’s just fire 🔥

It really good I like the character magpie she new and interesting also using your mic to blow the candles out and the guard can actually hear you voice that really cool there are some bugs but I don't mind I can't wait to play more

I LOVE this game, but everyday i am fiddling with restores of savefiles from the cloud and reboots of the Quest 3s in order to play from the middle of missions. 

It keeps laoding me into the clocktower at the start of a mission. Lost progress for HOURS. 

Only after fiddling for an hour i am loaded into the middle of my game.

Infuriating and unforgivable bug!!!!!!!!!

Edit after Patch 2.0:
After this patch i had to start my latest mission anew. Did't load my savegame, again back to the clocktower.  Ok, well, no biggy.
And then comes Stonemarket part 2 (after being jailed)... Game keeps crashing when i enter the room where my lockpicks are. 

The release of this game is a complete JOKE! Unforgivable mess. Love everything about it except for the enormous amount of BUGS, BUGS, BUGS!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi. Thank you for playing and sharing your feedback. We've just released a new update this week that addresses many issues, including significant improvements to the save system.
Please update to the latest version and let us know if this solves your issues.

reviews.review_description: I so wanted for this to be good, it could have been great. I have a long history with the their franchise. The franchise lends itself really well to the possibility of a vr game, there’s the “thief the dark project” which I would have hoped the devs had looked at before this. Now the story is good, and some of the mechanics of the game work well, but the bow isn’t one of them. To aim properly you have to occlude one controller and then they drift . The guard ai is not great either and there no way you’d take down a fully armoured guard with just a black jack. The game is shorter than I’d like too. The real issues is the number of bugs, from being stuck in the black void when pressing continue to load your previous save, the weird clipping issues and massive stuttering and frame drops  in some areas. I’ve not played the pcvr version so don’t know if this there in that but  frame drops are really .  This could have been such a good game but it misses the mark, I hope a patch addresses issues and I’d love it if they allowed modding like the original games for new missions etc but I’m not hopeful. If you’re considering buying this hold fire and read the reviews carefully before committing any cash.
Super fun game that I missed as soon as I was done with it! It isn't weeks long gameplay but if you do it right and try to get all the achievements, it is hard but fun  😉

developer_response.body: Thank you for the positive review!

reviews.review_description:
The game started to hang forever while loading. Restarting my quest 3 didn't help. I then tried restoring a cloud save from yesterday which got the game to load, but now my save file is gone! After many hours of playing I've lost all my progress. Can the developer fix this bug so I don't have to start the game over? I play a lot of games on my quest 3 and this is the only game that has done this.

UPDATE: I was able to partially work around this by restoring a previous save file from the cloud so at least I don't need to replay the whole game. Apparently the latest save file was corrupted. This is a good game, so it's disappointing to have frustrating bugs like this.

developer_response.body: Hi. Thanks for playing. Sorry to hear you had issues with the game. In our last few updates we're made improvements to many elements of the game, including the save system. We hope that this makes your experience better. If you still experience issues or have any other feedback please let us know.

reviews.review_description:
Thief in VR is a superb way to play. From combat to climbing, picking locks to picking pockets, everything feels great.

One star off for being very short, an easy endgame, a few glitches with assets disappearing and objects not graspable, and being able to easily hide in full moonlight.

This release feels like it was rushed out before it was ready.  Feels like everything was done as cheaply as possible just to rush a product out the door. Countless bugs and graphical issues ranging from silly to annoying and game breaking and blocking progress or requiring you restart and lose progress. 

As an example I have had floating doors. Dead bodies floating in space. Dead guards respawn as standing static inactive models. 

Then there is just the overall lack of basic things you would expect. Like the crow having zero collision. Or the way keys can't just be entered into locks and turned you have to do it in two seperate stages. 

Overall level design is decent but enemy AI is stupid making actual stealth feel pointless or trivial. Not enough to make you want to replay levels. Atmosphere is non existent with everything being very overly bright compared with the PC version. At no point do you feel you are in darkness or shadow. 

Pretty disappointed with this. Other than it borrowing visuals from the thief game it's a pretty bland and generic VR game that deserved to be much better presented.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thank you for your review. Sorry to hear you had a less than ideal experience with the game. We've since released a number of updates, including our latest patch just yesterday, that should address a number of your complaints. Please let us know your thoughts on the game with the improvements and fixes.

Cheers

reviews.review_title: A Wonderful Return to the World of Thief
reviews.review_description: The original Thief series is one of my favorite series. VR games tend to be smaller and shorter than PC-based games and this is no exception, but I really enjoyed this and hope for sequels. There are still a couple of bugs here and there, invisible doors and things like that, but a restart from checkpoint seems to clear them. Thanks for bringing Thief to VR :)

I want to start off by saying I love the idea, the whole sneaking around and just lurking in the shadows but ..it kept glitching on me, I would be still, not pressing any buttons and it would move me or bump me into something. The bow is is also way off. I'm not great at it but not thaat bad 😞 it was very hard to grab things or simply turn the key. It should wouldn't turn my hand. So all these things ruined it for me. Maybe its my headset specifically but yeah it just felt klunky and old does that make sense? Maybe I'll come back and try again. I really did want to love it

reviews.review_title: The Thief is great,I like it.
reviews.review_description: The Thief series is great, I have played it on the computer since I was a child. Unexpectedly, this time I finally entered the world of Thief. The graphics are great. Keep up the good work! ! ! Must have 5 star rating
